仕組み
カラーネガの仕組み 反転パイプライン 濃度と対数空間 トーン曲線（Faithfulルック） 色彩とホワイトバランス フィルムベースのキャリブレーション

ドキュメント
このページはまだ翻訳されていません — 英語版を表示しています。

OpenEnlarge Documentation

OpenEnlarge inverts color-negative film scans in the density domain — the same Beer–Lambert chemistry a darkroom enlarger relies on, not a flipped tone curve. These docs explain the science and every tool.

Start here

  • How inversion works — scan → density → positive, stage by stage.
  • Why negatives are orange — the film base and the H&D curve.
  • What am I tuning? — every Basic-panel slider, demystified.
  • The workflow — from import to export.

The idea in one paragraph

A color negative stores the scene as density on three dye layers, behind an orange mask. OpenEnlarge measures that density in logarithmic space, removes the mask, reconstructs the positive with a film-faithful tone curve, and only then applies your edits. Because the math matches the physics of film and paper, colors land where they should — across a whole roll, consistently.

OpenEnlarge — オープンソースのフィルムスキャン編集 · MITライセンス
